test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_suburban_home
test_cases.scenario: Generate complete emergency plan for typical suburban family with 4 occupants, no pets, no special medical needs, in moderate-risk flood zone.

input.home_description: 2-story suburban house in San Jose, California. 4 occupants: 2 adults (ages 35, 38), 2 children (ages 8, 12). No pets. No medical conditions or mobility constraints. Home has 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, approximately 2000 sq ft. Located 2 miles from Highway 101.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all 6 phases successfully. Generates complete emergency preparedness plan with validated home profile, supply list with documented quantities (56 gallons water for 4 occupants × 14 days), evacuation routes with text directions and meeting points with GPS coordinates, communication protocol with household contact list and out-of-area contact, insurance checklist flagging flood insurance gap, and formatted final document with all appendices.

test_cases.name: happy_path_family_with_pets_and_medical_needs
test_cases.scenario: Generate emergency plan for family with 3 occupants including elderly parent with diabetes and mobility constraint, 2 dogs, in wildfire-prone area.

input.home_description: Single-story ranch home in Sonoma County, California. 3 occupants: 1 adult (age 45), 1 elderly parent (age 78, uses walker, has Type 2 diabetes requiring insulin and glucose monitoring), 1 teenager (age 16). 2 medium-sized dogs (golden retrievers, 60 lbs each). Home has 3 bedrooms, 1.5 bathrooms, approximately 1500 sq ft. Located in area with high wildfire risk.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all phases with special attention to medical needs and accessibility. Home profile captures diabetes, insulin storage, glucose monitoring equipment, and walker requirement. Supply list includes 42 gallons water (3 occupants × 14 days), 30-day insulin supply, glucose monitor with test strips, N95 masks for wildfire, and 30-day dog food supply. Evacuation plan includes accessible routes for walker, pet-friendly shelters with contact info, and transportation assistance for elderly occupant. Communication protocol includes medical information sheet for elderly parent. Insurance checklist recommends comprehensive coverage given medical needs.

test_cases.name: edge_case_vague_home_description
test_cases.scenario: Test handling of minimal, vague home description that lacks specific location, occupant count, and medical details.

input.home_description: A house with some people and a few health issues. Somewhere in California.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill adjusts gracefully to vague input. Home profile includes clarification_needed array with specific requests: location (address or neighborhood), occupant count (exact number), medical details (medication names, dosages, equipment). Uses conservative estimate of 4 occupants and generic first aid supplies. Evacuation plan notes placeholder location and requests specific address. Final plan includes action items to gather missing information before plan is finalized.

test_cases.name: edge_case_single_occupant_apartment
test_cases.scenario: Test handling of minimal household: single occupant in apartment with very limited storage space.

input.home_description: Studio apartment in downtown San Francisco. 1 occupant (age 32, no medical conditions, no pets). Approximately 400 sq ft with minimal storage space (only 1 small closet).

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates plan scaled for single occupant and limited storage. Supply list shows 14 gallons water (1 occupant × 14 days) and flags storage constraint: "Supplies require X cubic feet; available storage is limited. Options: store some supplies at neighbor's apartment, use under-bed storage, rotate supplies more frequently." Evacuation plan identifies building meeting point and nearby public shelter. Insurance checklist recommends earthquake insurance for renter. Plan emphasizes compact, stackable supplies.

test_cases.name: edge_case_large_household_with_infants
test_cases.scenario: Test handling of large household with 8 occupants including 2 infants requiring specialized supplies.

input.home_description: 4-bedroom house in Portland, Oregon. 8 occupants: 2 adults (ages 40, 42), 4 children (ages 14, 11, 6, 4), 2 infants (ages 2 and 6 months). No pets. 1 child has severe peanut allergy (carries EpiPen). Home approximately 2500 sq ft.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates plan for large household with specialized needs. Supply list shows 112 gallons water (8 occupants × 14 days), includes infant formula (30-day supply), diapers, peanut-free foods, and 2 EpiPens. Evacuation plan addresses transportation for 2 infants and 4 children with specific assistance plan. Communication protocol includes teaching children out-of-area contact number, laminated contact cards for children, and note about peanut allergy for emergency responders. Insurance checklist recommends comprehensive coverage for large household.

test_cases.name: error_handling_missing_location
test_cases.scenario: Test error handling when home description lacks specific location information needed for evacuation route mapping.

input.home_description: A house somewhere in California with 4 people. No other details provided.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill adjusts gracefully when location is missing. Home profile flags location in clarification_needed array: "Cannot map evacuation routes without specific address or neighborhood." Evacuation plan proceeds with generic evacuation principles (e.g., "evacuate away from flood zones, toward higher ground") and includes note: "Specific address not provided. Evacuation routes are generic; update with specific routes once address is confirmed." Plan is still generated but marked as incomplete pending location information.

test_cases.name: error_handling_conflicting_medical_and_storage
test_cases.scenario: Test error handling when medical supply needs exceed available storage space in home.

input.home_description: Small studio apartment in Seattle, 1 occupant (age 55, Type 1 diabetes requiring insulin pump, continuous glucose monitor, backup insulin pens, and supplies). Approximately 300 sq ft with minimal storage (only 1 small closet and under-sink cabinet).

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies storage conflict and flags it prominently in supply_list. Message: "Medical supplies require X cubic feet; available storage is Y cubic feet. Options: (1) store some supplies at neighbor's apartment, (2) rotate supplies more frequently, (3) request occupant to reduce medication supply to 2 weeks instead of 4 weeks. Verify with occupant." Plan includes action item to resolve storage conflict. Evacuation plan includes note about critical medical supplies that must be taken first.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_insurance_coverage_gap_flood_zone
test_cases.scenario: Test quality gate for insurance checklist when home is in FEMA flood zone but has no flood insurance.

input.home_description: 2-story house in Houston, Texas, in FEMA flood zone AE (high-risk area). 4 occupants (2 adults, 2 children). Home valued at $350,000.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill identifies critical insurance gap and flags it prominently in insurance_checklist as high-priority action. Message: "Home is in FEMA flood zone AE. Flood insurance is REQUIRED. Action: Contact insurance agent within 30 days to add coverage. Estimated premium: $500-2000/year depending on risk level." Insurance checklist includes specific action item with due date (30 days). Final plan emphasizes this action item as critical before plan is considered complete.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_accessibility_evacuation_routes
test_cases.scenario: Test quality gate for evacuation plan when occupant with mobility constraint (wheelchair user) has accessible routes identified.

input.home_description: Single-story accessible apartment in Los Angeles. 2 occupants: 1 adult (age 45, wheelchair user, uses accessible van for transportation), 1 adult (age 48, no mobility constraints). Apartment has accessible entrance, accessible parking, accessible bathroom. Located 0.5 miles from accessible public shelter.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates evacuation plan with verified accessible routes. Evacuation plan includes: primary route verified as wheelchair-accessible (no stairs, ramps present, accessible parking at destination), secondary route also verified as accessible, shelter location confirmed as wheelchair-accessible with accessible parking and accessible restrooms. Accessibility notes specify: "Wheelchair user will evacuate using accessible van. Primary route: [accessible route with specific landmarks]. Shelter at [location] is wheelchair-accessible with accessible parking and restrooms." Transportation assistance plan specifies who will drive accessible van and backup plan if primary driver unavailable.

test_cases.name: quality_gate_communication_protocol_non_english_speaker
test_cases.scenario: Test quality gate for communication protocol when household includes non-English speaker with identified translation resources.

input.home_description: House in San Jose, California. 3 occupants: 1 adult (age 50, Spanish-speaking only, no English), 1 adult (age 48, bilingual English/Spanish), 1 child (age 10, bilingual English/Spanish). Home has 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill generates communication protocol with specific translation accommodations. Household contact list includes accessibility_needs field noting "Spanish-speaking occupant; primary communication in Spanish." Special communication needs section specifies: "Spanish-speaking occupant will receive instructions in Spanish. Bilingual family member (Adult B) will help translate emergency broadcasts. Neighbor Maria (555-5678) speaks Spanish and can provide additional translation support. Emergency services can be contacted; request Spanish-speaking responder." Wallet card includes Spanish translations of key information (out-of-area contact, meeting points, emergency procedures). Plan includes action item to verify translation resources are available before emergency.
